title: Direct Routing and Bounded Worker Pools · Language Lens: Task Cost Fairness
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must combine direct routing with bounded goroutines, prefetch, dependency capacity, and acknowledgement after durable effect; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the task cost fairness increment in a capacity-aware Go work distribution service. Learners progress…
objectives: Measure service time, prefetch, unacknowledged work, capacity, and tenant fairness instead of equating round robin with fair completion; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use one queue with multiple consumers for load distribution and state that each delivery goes to one active consumer at a time; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Task Cost Fairness · Theory Task Cost Fairness
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 3769 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure service time, prefetch, unacknowledged work, capacity, and tenant fairness instead of equating round robin with fair completion; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
